events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Image shows lookahead schedule early-start bars. Activities with durations mostly. D duration illegible. Disruption: Activity D (Framing) needs 2 extra workdays. Need determine project delay caused by disruption, or state missing info. Need compute project delay. Since D duration unknown, but maybe can infer from bar length? The bar for D from day 10 to ~14? Let's inspect image. D Framing [?] <- CC. CC cure from ~7 to 10 (3d). D bar starts at 10, ends at maybe 14? It looks blue bar from 10 to 14 (duration 4?). But printed duration illegible. The schedule shows early-start bars. Need compute delay. If D is on critical path? Let's map network. Predecessors: A->B->C->CC->D->E->F/G/H->I->J->K->L->M->N->O/P->Q. There are parallel paths after E: F(3d), G(4d), H(5d) all to I. So critical path likely through H (longest 5d). D duration affects E start and subsequent critical path. Since D is predecessor to E only, and E is on critical path? Let's compute original durations to find project duration and critical path. Need D original duration. Could infer from bar length: D bar from day 10 to day 14? Actually x-axis ticks every 5 with minor grid maybe every 1. D bar appears to start at 10 and end at 14 (length 4). But printed [?]. The question likely expects: cannot compute exact delay because D's original duration is illegible; need that duration. But maybe can infer from schedule geometry. Let's examine carefully.

Image: x-axis 0 to 40+ with ticks at 0,5,10,15,20,... Grid lines every 1 maybe faint. Activity bars:
A at 0-1 (1d)
B 1-3 (2d)
C 3-7 (4d)
CC 7-10 (3d cure)
D 10-? The blue bar has a question mark inside. It seems to end at 14 (since next E starts at 15). E 15-17 (2d). F 17-20 (3d), G 17-21 (4d), H 17-22 (5d). I 22-23 (1d inspection). J 23-26 (3d). K 26-27 (1d). L 27-32 (5d). M 32-38 (6d). N 38-40 (2d). O 40-42 (2d), P 40-42 (2d). Q 42-43 (1d). So D duration 4d (10-14). E starts at 15? Wait if D ends at 14, E starts 15. But early start bars maybe start next day. D bar from 10 inclusive to 14 inclusive? Duration 4. E starts at 15. That matches.

If D original duration is 4d, and disruption adds 2d => D becomes 6d, finishing day 16 (if start day 10, duration 6 => ends day 16). Then E starts day 17. H path: H 17-22 becomes? Actually if E starts 17, E 17-19, F 17-20, G 17-21, H 17-22. I 22-23, J 23-26, K 26-27, L 27-32, M 32-38, N 38-40, O/P 40-42, Q 42-43. So project finish shifts from day 43 to day 45? Wait original finish Q at 43? Let's compute original: Q final inspection at 42-43. Project duration 43 workdays (finish at end of day 43). With D +2, Q 44-45? Let's recalc: D finish day 16 (start 10, duration 6). E start 17, finish 18 (duration 2). H start 19? Wait E is predecessor to F,G,H. If E finishes day 18, H starts day 19, duration 5 => finishes day 23. I starts day 24, finishes 24. J 25-27. K 28. L 29-33. M 34-39. N 40-41. O/P 42-43. Q 44. Hmm need be careful with start/end conventions.

Original schedule: D starts day 10, duration 4 => finishes day 13? But bar shows to 14. Let's define workdays as continuous intervals. If A [0,1) etc. But schedule bars likely represent start at left edge, end at right edge, duration = length. D from 10 to 14 => duration 4. E starts at 14? Actually bar E starts at 15. There may be a 1-day lag? No, D->E no lag. If D finishes at end of day 14, E starts day 15. So early start = successor start = predecessor finish +1. Fine.

Original D finish = 14. E start =15, E finish=16 (duration 2). F start=16? Wait if E finishes 16, F/G/H start 17? But bars show F starts at 17, G/H at 17. E bar 15-17 (finish at 17). So E finish =17. Then F start 17 finish 20, G
